cinematic_pitch: Trace the gentle contours of Florida’s Gulf Coast where asphalt ribbons along a coastal highway framed by serene lakes and low, verdant hills. This easy, coastal ride offers a refined blend of tranquil water vistas and subtle elevation changes, inviting riders to explore a landscape punctuated by Florida’s highest peak and shimmering inland lakes.
ride_character: A smooth, coastal highway ride with effortless curves and an easy grade, weaving past tranquil lakes and modest hills that offer a refreshing, contemplative rhythm.
best_season: February through May — when mild temperatures and low humidity create perfect conditions for relaxed daytime cruising along the coast and lakes.

premium_pois.name: Sugarloaf Mountain
premium_pois.cinematic_description: Rising to 312 meters, Sugarloaf Mountain is the highest point in peninsular Florida, providing an unexpected elevation amidst the coastal terrain and a rewarding vantage point for panoramic views.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Plan a stop here to stretch legs and enjoy the unique perspective—gear down for the gentle ascent leading to the peak.

premium_pois.name: Lake Morton
premium_pois.cinematic_description: Nestled within Lakeland, Lake Morton invites riders to pause and absorb its placid surface reflecting the sky, a serene contrast to the coastal breeze and asphalt beneath your wheels.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Use local parking areas to safely pull over and savor the lakeside ambiance without disrupting the steady flow of traffic.

premium_pois.name: Lake Horney
premium_pois.cinematic_description: Located in Polk County, Lake Horney offers a quiet, reflective retreat surrounded by gentle terrain, enhancing the sense of calm that defines this Gulf Coast journey.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Take care navigating nearby turns as the road skirts close to the lake’s edge, ensuring a smooth and attentive ride.
